steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student wants to compare tribal economic activity with two other industries across three table metrics: total contribution, employment, and average contribution per employee. The correct choice must be a claim that the table data actually supports.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "ranked highest in all three economic measures listed in the table.": tribal activity does not rank highest in all three. Professional services exceeds it in total contribution (110,157 dollars vs. tribal's figure), and construction exceeds it in employment (77,247 vs. 51,674). ✗
  • "employed the same number of people as construction.": tribal employment is 51,674; construction's is 77,247. These are not the same. ✗
  • "made the lowest total contribution by industry.": construction's total contribution (6.8 billion dollars) is lower than tribal's (7.3 billion dollars), so tribal is not the lowest. ✗
  • "made the highest average economic contribution per employee.": tribal's average of 141,510 dollars per employee beats professional services (110,157 dollars) and construction (87,994 dollars). This is the one metric where tribal ranks first. ✓
That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
